#  Assign Default Cost Types To ERP Standard Cost Codes 
##### ERP DOCUMENTATION
**Each Procore-built ERP integration offers its own unique feature set.** Your integration might not support the action described on this page.
To learn about your integration and its supported features, see [Which ERP integrations are supported by Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-erp-integrations-are-supported-by-procore)
##  Background 
In the construction industry, _cost types_ are often assigned to cost codes to identify the specific work being performed on a construction project. To help project teams better account for their costs (especially their internal costs), it is a common practice to assign multiple cost types to a single cost code.
To understand the relationship between these two objects, keep in mind:
  * A _Cost Code_ is a code that defines the specific type of work being completed on a construction project. When using an Integrated ERP System with Procore, cost codes are synced to individual segment items (a.k.a., 'cost codes' on Procore's 'Cost Code' segment, which is a tiered segment. To learn more, see [What are Procore's default cost codes?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-procores-default-cost-codes)
  * A _Cost Type_ is a set of costs identified by a unique abbreviation or label. In Procore's WBS, 'Cost Type' is a flat segment with these default options: _(E) Equipment, (L) Labor, (M) Materials, (O) Other, (OC) Owner Cost, (S) Commitments,_ and _(SVC) Professional Services._ See [What are Procore's default cost types?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-procores-default-cost-types)


You must first add Cost Codes to the ERP standard cost codes list in the company Admin tool before assigning default cost types in the ERP Integrations tool.
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions** :
    * 'Standard' or 'Admin' level permissions on the company's ERP Integrations tool.
  * **Additional Information** :
    * You can assign one or more cost type(s) to each standard cost code. For example, both L=Labor and E=Equipment.
  * **Not all integrations support the cost type concept.**


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the company's **ERP Integrations** tool.
  2. Click the **Std. Cost Codes & Cost Types** tab.
  3. For each ERP standard cost code in your company's list, mark one (1) or more checkboxes to assign the code to one (1) or more cost types.


##### NOTE
  * To apply a specific cost type to all of the cost codes in the list, double-click the desired cost type column.
  * You can hover your mouse cursor over the column headings, to reveal a tooltip that shows the full name for the cost type.![View Erp Standard Cost Codes And Cost Types Tab](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/9cc4d48a98db78f6162788c814789072/205df8e24f4c09d1b6769f2f96c6e30a/view-erp-standard-cost-codes-and-cost-types-tab.png?fm=webp)
  * The cost type assignments are automatically saved.


## See Also
  * [Add a Procore Project to your ERP System](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/erp-integrations-company/tutorials/add-a-procore-project-to-your-erp-system)
  * [Configure Cost Code Preferences for ERP](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/erp-integrations-company/tutorials/configure-cost-code-preferences-for-erp)
  * [Refresh the Cost Codes on an ERP Integrated Project](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/erp-integrations-company/tutorials/refresh-the-cost-codes-on-an-erp-integrated-project)


In Procore, the term _integrated ERP System_ , is used to refer to _Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)_ software applications that can be configured to exchange data between Procore and a supported application using the Company level ERP Integrations tool. See [Which ERP integrations are supported by Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-erp-integrations-are-supported-by-procore)
title: Assign Default Cost Types To ERP Standard Cost Codes
description: To assign default 'cost types' to ERP standard cost codes.
